message:
bd-2ki: Add extc compatibility contract to EXTENSIONS.md

Adds §2A "Extc Compatibility Contract (Normative)" with:
- Canonical import rewrite rules (node:* and bare builtins to pi:node/*)
- Global polyfill injection rules
- Forbidden/flagged API classification with error taxonomy
- Full compatibility matrix (fs, path, os, crypto, child_process, etc.)
- Sourcemap contract for accurate error locations
- Test requirements for E2E harness validation

Also closes bd-fww (model serialization tests - 48 tests in
tests/model_serialization.rs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-27q2","title":"Editor: styling parity (thinking-level indicator + bash mode)","description":"# Goal
Match small but important legacy editor affordances:
- Editor border color indicates current thinking level.
- When editor content starts with `!`, show bash-mode styling.

# Notes
Rust interactive currently renders a simple input region without border styling.
Implement the closest equivalent (border, accent color, or label) without overhauling the UI.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can visually distinguish thinking level at a glance.
- [ ] Bash-mode input is visually indicated.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:54:20.055650169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:54:20.066465191Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27q2","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:54:20.066422121Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27t","title":"E2E CLI scenarios (print/continue/resume/export/packages) with artifact logging","description":"# Goal
Own the CLI E2E suite as a rollup: coordinate scenario tasks and ensure unified logging + artifact capture.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Aggregate scenario tasks: print/stdin (bd-1ub), session lifecycle (bd-idw), tool enable/disable + error paths (bd-1o4).
- Ensure shared harness/env isolation and logging spec are applied consistently.
- Provide a single entrypoint script/runner to execute the full CLI E2E suite and collect artifacts.

# Acceptance
- All child scenario tasks completed and wired into a single CLI E2E suite run.
- Logs follow unified JSONL spec and artifact index.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:51.748950351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:36.774727141Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1o4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:33.315741534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:29.253315707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:35.326572813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-c4q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:51.762098761Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-idw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:31.281340866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Expand CLI E2E: print mode, @file context, --continue/--resume, session export HTML, package list/install/remove (local/global), config paths, model listing. Capture stdout/stderr + timing + session artifacts per run.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:20:17Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rollup note: treat bd-1ub, bd-idw, bd-1o4 as the scenario tasks; this issue owns the unified CLI E2E runner and ensures JSONL log spec (bd-4u9) is applied across all scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28rk","title":"Autocomplete: core provider (commands/prompts/skills/files/paths)","description":"# Goal
Implement the core autocomplete provider that produces suggestions based on editor text + cursor position.

# Suggestions required (legacy parity)
- Slash commands: built-in commands (`/help`, `/model`, `/settings`, etc.).
- Prompt templates: `/<template>` from ResourceLoader.
- Skills: `/skill:<name>` when enableSkillCommands is true.
- File reference: when user types `@`, fuzzy-search project files and insert selected path.
- Path completion: when cursor is in a path-like token, produce candidates (use `fd` if available).

# Matching
- Fuzzy matching for file and command lists.
- Stable ordering (deterministic): exact prefix matches first, then fuzzy score.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep provider logic independent of rendering.
- Provider should return a structured `AutocompleteItem`:
  - display label
  - insert text
  - optional description
  - kind (command/template/skill/file/path)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provider can be called with (text, cursor) and returns stable suggestions.
- [ ] Provider includes file suggestions without scanning the entire filesystem on every keystroke (cache or incremental search).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.177076040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441887518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:17:45.441818900Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28rk","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:08.186267387Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-28t8","title":"Autocomplete: UI dropdown + selection/insertion","description":"# Goal
Add an interactive autocomplete dropdown to the editor, matching the core UX of legacy Pi.

# Required Behavior
- Trigger conditions:
  - typing `/` shows command/template suggestions
  - typing `@` shows file suggestions
  - Tab can also open/advance path completion
- Navigation:
  - Up/Down (or configured selection keys)
  - Enter/Tab to accept
  - Escape/Ctrl+C to cancel
- Display:
  - max visible items respects `autocomplete_max_visible` config
  - show item kind (icon or color) and optional description

# Implementation Notes
- Use bubbles list-like UI or a small custom component.
- Keep insertion logic robust:
  - insert at cursor
  - replace the active token prefix (e.g., replace `@foo` with `@src/foo.rs` or just `src/foo.rs` depending on chosen syntax)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Autocomplete can be opened, navigated, accepted, cancelled.
- [ ] Acceptance inserts the correct text at the correct location.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:18.250299287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:38.349040279Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:18.259401568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-28rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:37.738490671Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28t8","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:38.349011966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Add an interactive autocomplete dropdown to the editor, matching the core UX of legacy Pi.

 {"id":"bd-28uy","title":"Interactive: /copy copies last assistant message to clipboard","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/copy` behavior: copy last assistant message text to the system clipboard.

# Required Behavior
- If there is no assistant message yet: show an error (“No agent messages to copy yet.” or equivalent).
- On success: show a short status (“Copied …”).
- On failure: show an actionable error (clipboard unavailable).

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a cross-platform clipboard crate with minimal optional dependencies.
- Avoid feature-gating the entire command away; at minimum provide a runtime fallback that prints the text or writes it to a temp file with a clear message.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/copy` works out-of-the-box on macOS/Linux/Windows where clipboard is available.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.932575094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.938838364Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28uy","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.938806395Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29c","title":"Workstream: popular extension sample selection + acquisition complete","description":"Purpose:
- Produce a *large, diverse, popular* extension sample with frozen versions and archived artifacts so conformance tests are repeatable.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Sample selection criteria + sampling matrix (documented).
- Final manifest of chosen extensions (name, source, version, checksum, capabilities, categories).
- Archived artifacts (tarballs/zip) stored under a dedicated fixtures area with checksums.

Definition of done:
- Sample list is final and frozen with versions.
- Artifacts are locally archived with checksums.
- License/redistribution policy applied to each artifact (OK / restricted / exclude).

Dependencies:
- This workstream blocks legacy capture, harness, and reporting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:44.802403431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:28:15.393814835Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-22h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:48.803635894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-2uv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:27.483930216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-2wo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:56.575435375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-3rr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:28:15.393785220Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-gx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:40.976426571Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-29c","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:28:06.592012396Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b3","title":"Resource loader + package manager tests with real fixtures","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:12.631899363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:19:34.391872711Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b3","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:12.639492130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b3","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:12.643145227Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-2b3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cover resource discovery precedence (project > global), CLI overrides (--extension/--no-extensions), and manifest-driven resources. Use real temp package roots with package.json/pi fields and verify diagnostics for collisions/missing assets.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:19:34Z"}]}
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2jdz","title":"Interactive: implement /new (start new session without restart)","description":"# Goal
Implement `/new` inside interactive mode to start a fresh session without restarting the process.

# Required Behavior
- Clear the current conversation view.
- Create a new Session:
  - new session id
  - new JSONL file path (if persistence enabled)
  - header uses current cwd, model/thinking settings
- Reset agent message history to an empty session context.

# UX
- Preserve loaded resources (skills/prompts/themes) from current run.
- Provide a status message indicating new session started.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/new` results in a clean slate session.
- [ ] Persistence works (new file created, not overwriting old).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:34.248604864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484206305Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484141665Z","close_reason":"Implemented /new interactive command; resets conversation + new session persistence","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jdz","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:34.255254144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure for API error testing)

## Files
- tests/error_handling.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 25+ error handling tests
- [ ] All network errors covered (via VCR/local simulation)
- [ ] All API error codes covered
- [ ] File system errors covered
- [ ] Tool errors covered
- [ ] Logs include error category + cassette id
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.110276433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:34.660912407Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:41:01.345001879Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.123723281Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:34.660884836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:32.812857475Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ke","title":"Impl: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics","description":"# Goal
Wire QuickJS async runtime to the PiJS event loop with a **correct Promise/hostcall bridge** and explicit job draining.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `host.call` returns a JS Promise backed by a Rust completion handle.
- Job draining loop: run pending jobs, then poll timers/hostcalls per spec.
- Cancellation propagation: cancel token to hostcall, reject Promise with mapped error.
- Explicit error mapping + stack traces for debugging.
- Structured logs per hostcall with correlation IDs.

# Correctness Notes
- No re-entrancy hazards: only drain jobs at safe points.
- Promise resolution order stable with scheduler sequence ids.

# Tests
- Unit tests for Promise resolution ordering.
- Conformance fixtures for cancellation/timeouts.
- E2E lifecycle harness asserts log ordering.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:13.010362542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:06:43.680593840Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:47.700708791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.163859792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:48.673842173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

Implementation beads:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0
- node:child_process subset: bd-2sr
- WebAssembly bridge (PiWasm): bd-1ry

# Tests
- Unit transform fixtures for common imports and injection.
- Negative tests for forbidden APIs with exact messages.
- E2E harness verifies rewritten bundles run 16/16 with actionable failures.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics (bd-123) and hostcall ABI (bd-37z).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:59.736950487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:20:07.166640123Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.067354913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.533406188Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

 {"id":"bd-2lg","title":"Implement compaction module unit tests","description":"# Implement compaction module unit tests

## Goal
Add comprehensive unit tests for src/compaction.rs (903 lines, currently 0 tests).

## Background
Compaction is a critical feature that:
- Summarizes old conversation history to save context tokens
- Identifies optimal cut points in message history
- Tracks file operations (read/write/edit) for summary
- Uses LLM to generate summaries

## Module Analysis (src/compaction.rs)

### Key Types
```rust
struct CompactionContext {
    entries: Vec<SessionEntry>,
    config: CompactionConfig,
    token_estimator: TokenEstimator,
}

struct CutPoint {
    entry_index: usize,
    reason: CutPointReason,
    tokens_before: usize,
    tokens_after: usize,
}

enum CutPointReason {
    TokenLimit,
    UserMessageBoundary,
    ToolResultBoundary,
    NaturalBreak,
}

struct FileSummary {
    path: String,
    operations: Vec<FileOp>,
}
```

### Key Functions
- `estimate_tokens(entry: &SessionEntry) -> usize`
- `find_cut_points(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> Vec<CutPoint>`
- `extract_file_operations(entries: &[SessionEntry]) -> Vec<FileSummary>`
- `prepare_compaction(entries: &[SessionEntry], config: &Config) -> CompactionPlan`
- `generate_summary_prompt(entries: &[SessionEntry], files: &[FileSummary]) -> String`

## Test Categories

### 1. Token Estimation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_simple_text() {
    let entry = user_message("Hello, world!");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    assert!(tokens > 0 && tokens < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_code_block() {
    let entry = assistant_message("```rust\nfn main() {}\n```");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Code blocks should estimate higher
}

#[test]
fn test_estimate_tokens_tool_result() {
    let entry = tool_result("read", "Large file content...");
    let tokens = estimate_tokens(&entry);
    // Verify reasonable estimate
}
```

### 2. Cut Point Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_find_cut_points_token_limit() {
    let entries = generate_entries(100, 1000);  // 100 entries, ~1000 tokens each
    let config = CompactionConfig { max_tokens: 50000 };
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should find cuts when exceeding limit
    assert!(!cuts.is_empty());
    assert!(cuts[0].tokens_after <= config.max_tokens);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_prefers_user_message_boundary() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Q1"),
        assistant_message("A1"),
        user_message("Q2"),  // Preferred cut point
        assistant_message("A2"),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &low_limit_config());
    
    // Should cut at user message boundary
    assert_eq!(cuts[0].reason, CutPointReason::UserMessageBoundary);
}

#[test]
fn test_cut_point_preserves_tool_pairs() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Read file"),
        assistant_with_tool_call("read", { "path": "test.txt" }),
        tool_result("read", "File contents..."),
        assistant_message("The file contains..."),
    ];
    
    let cuts = find_cut_points(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should not cut between tool call and result
    for cut in cuts {
        assert_ne!(cut.entry_index, 2);  // Not after tool call
    }
}
```

### 3. File Operation Extraction Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_read() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "src/main.rs", "lines_read": 100})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(files[0].path, "src/main.rs");
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Read { lines: 100 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_write() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("write", json!({"path": "new.txt", "bytes_written": 1234})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert_eq!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Write { bytes: 1234 });
}

#[test]
fn test_extract_file_operations_edit() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({
            "path": "src/lib.rs",
            "old_length": 10,
            "new_length": 15
        })),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    assert!(matches!(files[0].operations[0], FileOp::Edit { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_file_operations_grouped_by_path() {
    let entries = vec![
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("edit", json!({"path": "a.txt"})),
        tool_result_with_details("read", json!({"path": "b.txt"})),
    ];
    
    let files = extract_file_operations(&entries);
    
    // Files should be deduplicated
    assert_eq!(files.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(files.iter().find(|f| f.path == "a.txt").unwrap().operations.len(), 2);
}
```

### 4. Summary Prompt Generation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_messages() {
    let entries = vec![
        user_message("Help me with X"),
        assistant_message("I'll help with X by doing Y"),
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&entries, &[]);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("Help me with X"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("Y"));
}

#[test]
fn test_summary_prompt_includes_file_summary() {
    let files = vec![
        FileSummary {
            path: "src/main.rs".into(),
            operations: vec![FileOp::Read { lines: 100 }, FileOp::Edit { old: 10, new: 15 }],
        },
    ];
    
    let prompt = generate_summary_prompt(&[], &files);
    
    assert!(prompt.contains("src/main.rs"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("read"));
    assert!(prompt.contains("edit"));
}
```

### 5. Compaction Plan Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_prepare_compaction_identifies_entries_to_keep() {
    let entries = generate_entries(50, 1000);
    let config = CompactionConfig {
        max_tokens: 30000,
        keep_recent: 10000,
    };
    
    let plan = prepare_compaction(&entries, &config);
    
    // Should keep recent entries
    assert!(plan.entries_to_keep.len() < entries.len());
    assert!(plan.tokens_after_compaction <= config.max_tokens);
}
```

## Dependencies
None (unit tests only)

## Files
- src/compaction.rs (add #[cfg(test)] module)

- [ ] Token estimation tested
- [ ] Cut point detection tested
- [ ] File operation extraction tested
- [ ] Summary prompt generation tested
- [ ] Edge cases covered

+## 2A. Extc Compatibility Contract (Normative)
+
+This section defines the **extc compiler contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports
+to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified**
+(no manual source edits required).
+
+### 2A.1 Genericity Constraint (Non-Negotiable)
+
+- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
+- Rewrites MUST be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic,
+  semantics-preserving code patterns.
+- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by
+  branching on extension id.
+
+### 2A.2 Canonical Import Rewrite Rules
+
+Extc MUST ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.
+
+#### A) Node Builtins (`node:*`)
+
+Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers
+don't externalize them):
+
+| Source Specifier        | Target Specifier           |
+|-------------------------|----------------------------|
+| `node:fs`               | `pi:node/fs`               |
+| `node:fs/promises`      | `pi:node/fs_promises`      |
+| `node:path`             | `pi:node/path`             |
+| `node:os`               | `pi:node/os`               |
+| `node:url`              | `pi:node/url`              |
+| `node:crypto`           | `pi:node/crypto`           |
+| `node:child_process`    | `pi:node/child_process`    |
+| `node:module`           | `pi:node/module`           |
+
+#### B) Bare Builtins (No Prefix)
+
+Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these
+identically:
+
+| Source Specifier        | Target Specifier           |
+|-------------------------|----------------------------|
+| `fs`                    | `pi:node/fs`               |
+| `fs/promises`           | `pi:node/fs_promises`      |
+| `path`                  | `pi:node/path`             |
+| `os`                    | `pi:node/os`               |
+| `url`                   | `pi:node/url`              |
+| `crypto`                | `pi:node/crypto`           |
+| `child_process`         | `pi:node/child_process`    |
+| `module`                | `pi:node/module`           |
+
+### 2A.3 Global Polyfill Injection
+
+Extc MAY inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
+
+```javascript
+import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'  // installs process, Buffer, __dirname, __filename
+import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'         // if needed: fetch, Headers, Request, Response
+import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'   // PiWasm bridge (QuickJS has no native wasm)
+```
+
+**Injection rules:**
+- **Deterministic**: stable ordering, always at the top of the entry module.
+- **Sourcemap-correct**: injected imports MUST NOT corrupt sourcemap line mappings.
+- **Versioned**: `shim_version` MUST be included in the artifact hash.
+- **Idempotent**: multiple injections produce identical output.
+
+**Node globals provided by `pi:polyfills/node_globals`:**
+- `process` (with `process.env`, `process.cwd()`, `process.platform`, etc.)
+- `Buffer`
+- `__dirname` / `__filename` (computed from module URL)
+- `global` (alias for `globalThis`)
+- `setImmediate` / `clearImmediate`
+
+### 2A.4 Forbidden and Flagged APIs
+
+The compatibility scanner MUST classify APIs into:
+
+#### Forbidden (Hard Error)
+
+APIs that bypass capability policy or escape the sandbox. Extc MUST reject bundles
+using these:
+
+| API / Pattern                        | Reason                                   |
+|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
+| `require('vm')` / `node:vm`          | Arbitrary code execution                 |
+| `require('worker_threads')`          | Unsupported concurrency model            |
+| `require('cluster')`                 | Unsupported concurrency model            |
+| `require('dgram')`                   | Raw UDP sockets                          |
+| `require('net')` (raw sockets)       | Bypasses HTTP policy                     |
+| `require('tls')` (raw sockets)       | Bypasses HTTP policy                     |
+| `require('inspector')`               | Debugger access                          |
+| `require('perf_hooks')`              | Performance timing oracle                |
+| `require('v8')`                      | Engine internals                         |
+| `require('repl')`                    | Interactive eval                         |
+| `process.binding()`                  | Native module access                     |
+| `process.dlopen()`                   | Native addon loading                     |
+| Direct `eval()` with dynamic string  | Arbitrary code execution (see note)      |
+
+**Note on `new Function(...)`:** The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for
+loading a bundled script. This is **flagged but allowed** with evidence logging,
+not forbidden outright.
+
+#### Flagged (Warning + Evidence)
+
+Risky constructs that require evidence logging but don't block compilation:
+
+| API / Pattern                        | Evidence Required                        |
+|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
+| `new Function(...)`                  | Log function body hash + call site       |
+| `eval(variable)`                     | Log if variable is not a literal         |
+| `setTimeout(string, ...)`            | Log string body hash                     |
+| `setInterval(string, ...)`           | Log string body hash                     |
+| `Proxy` / `Reflect` (reflection)     | Log usage pattern                        |
+| `Object.defineProperty` on builtins  | Log target + property                    |
+
+### 2A.5 Extc Input/Output Contract
+
+#### Input
+
+- Extension manifest (`extension.json` or `package.json`)
+- Source files (TypeScript or JavaScript)
+- Optional: `tsconfig.json` for type resolution
+
+#### Output
+
+- **ESM bundle**: single entry module, tree-shaken, minified
+- **Sourcemap**: accurate line/column mapping to original source
+- **Artifact metadata** (`artifact.json`):
+  ```json
+  {
+    "schema": "pi.ext.artifact.v1",
+    "extension_id": "...",
+    "entry_module": "index.js",
+    "hash": "sha256:...",
+    "shim_version": "1.0.0",
+    "rewrite_log": [
+      { "from": "node:fs", "to": "pi:node/fs", "locations": [...] }
+    ],
+    "injected_polyfills": ["pi:polyfills/node_globals"],
+    "flagged_apis": [
+      { "api": "new Function", "locations": [...], "evidence_hash": "..." }
+    ],
+    "forbidden_apis": [],
+    "capabilities_required": ["read", "exec"]
+  }
+  ```
+
+#### Side-Effect Policy
+
+- Extc MUST NOT execute extension code during compilation.
+- Static analysis only; no `require()` resolution that triggers side effects.
+- If a dependency cannot be statically analyzed, emit a warning and include it
+  verbatim (the runtime will handle capability checks).
+
+### 2A.6 Compatibility Matrix
+
+The following Node APIs are supported via shims. Each maps to a PiJS connector
+with explicit capability requirements:
+
+| Node API                 | Shim Module             | Sync/Async | Capability   | Notes                          |
+|--------------------------|-------------------------|------------|--------------|--------------------------------|
+| `fs.readFileSync`        | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `read`       | Blocks event loop              |
+| `fs.writeFileSync`       | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `write`      | Blocks event loop              |
+| `fs.promises.readFile`   | `pi:node/fs_promises`   | async      | `read`       | Preferred                      |
+| `fs.promises.writeFile`  | `pi:node/fs_promises`   | async      | `write`      | Preferred                      |
+| `fs.existsSync`          | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `read`       |                                |
+| `fs.readdirSync`         | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `read`       |                                |
+| `fs.statSync`            | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `read`       |                                |
+| `fs.mkdirSync`           | `pi:node/fs`            | sync       | `write`      |                                |
+| `path.join`              | `pi:node/path`          | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `path.resolve`           | `pi:node/path`          | sync       | (none)       | Uses `process.cwd()`           |
+| `path.dirname`           | `pi:node/path`          | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `path.basename`          | `pi:node/path`          | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `path.extname`           | `pi:node/path`          | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `os.platform`            | `pi:node/os`            | sync       | `env`        | Returns host platform          |
+| `os.homedir`             | `pi:node/os`            | sync       | `env`        | Returns home directory         |
+| `os.tmpdir`              | `pi:node/os`            | sync       | `env`        | Returns temp directory         |
+| `child_process.spawn`    | `pi:node/child_process` | async      | `exec`       | Streams stdout/stderr          |
+| `child_process.exec`     | `pi:node/child_process` | async      | `exec`       | Buffers output                 |
+| `child_process.execSync` | `pi:node/child_process` | sync       | `exec`       | Blocks; prefer async           |
+| `crypto.randomBytes`     | `pi:node/crypto`        | sync       | (none)       | CSPRNG                         |
+| `crypto.createHash`      | `pi:node/crypto`        | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `url.parse`              | `pi:node/url`           | sync       | (none)       | Pure computation               |
+| `url.URL`                | `pi:node/url`           | sync       | (none)       | WHATWG URL                     |
+| `process.env`            | `pi:polyfills/...`      | sync       | `env`        | Filtered by policy             |
+| `process.cwd()`          | `pi:polyfills/...`      | sync       | (none)       | Project root                   |
+| `process.exit()`         | `pi:polyfills/...`      | sync       | (none)       | Throws; extension cannot exit  |
+| `Buffer.from`            | `pi:polyfills/...`      | sync       | (none)       | Binary data handling           |
+| `Buffer.alloc`           | `pi:polyfills/...`      | sync       | (none)       | Binary data handling           |
+| `fetch`                  | `pi:polyfills/fetch`    | async      | `http`       | WHATWG Fetch                   |
+
+**Error mapping:** All shim errors MUST map to the hostcall error taxonomy (§3.2):
+`denied`, `timeout`, `io`, `invalid_request`, `internal`.
+
+### 2A.7 Sourcemap Contract
+
+Extc MUST produce sourcemaps that:
+
+1. **Map accurately**: every generated line/column MUST map to the correct original
+   source location.
+2. **Preserve through rewrites**: import rewrites MUST NOT corrupt mappings.
+3. **Include sources**: sourcemap SHOULD include `sourcesContent` for offline
+   debugging.
+4. **Inline or external**: support both inline (`//# sourceMappingURL=data:...`)
+   and external (`.map` file) formats.
+
+**Runtime usage:**
+- When an error occurs, the runtime MUST use the sourcemap to produce a stack trace
+  with original file/line/column.
+- Structured logs (§3.1) MUST include sourcemapped locations in `source.location`.
+
+### 2A.8 Test Requirements
+
+- **Unit transform fixtures**: common imports + injection patterns with expected
+  output.
+- **Negative tests**: forbidden APIs MUST produce exact error messages.
+- **E2E harness**: verify rewritten bundles run 16/16 sample extensions with
+  actionable failure diagnostics.
+
+---
+
 ## 3. Extension Protocol (v1)
 
 All communication uses a **versioned, JSON‑encoded protocol**:

+### 3.2A Unified JS + WASM Capability Model (Normative)
+
+This section defines a **single, coherent capability model** that applies
+equally to **PiJS (JS)** and **WASM** extensions. Policy evaluation, logging,
+and tooling **must not diverge** by runtime.
+
+#### Capability taxonomy (v1)
+
+| Capability | JS surface (PiJS) | WASM hostcall | Scope | Notes |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `read` | `pi.tool(read/grep/find/ls)`; `pi.fs.read/list/stat` | `host_call(method=tool, name in {read,grep,find,ls})`; `host_call(method=fs, op in {read,list,stat})` | `paths` | Path scope enforced by connector. |
+| `write` | `pi.tool(write/edit)`; `pi.fs.write/mkdir/delete` | `host_call(method=tool, name in {write,edit})`; `host_call(method=fs, op in {write,mkdir,delete})` | `paths` | Includes mutation; default-deny in strict mode. |
+| `exec` | `pi.exec(...)`; `pi.tool(bash)` | `host_call(method=exec)`; `host_call(method=tool, name=bash)` | none | Process execution; high-risk. |
+| `http` | `pi.http(request)` | `host_call(method=http)` | `hosts` | Host allow-list enforced. |
+| `session` | `pi.session.*` | `host_call(method=session)` | none | Session metadata access. |
+| `ui` | `pi.ui.*` | `host_call(method=ui)` | none | May be denied in non-interactive mode. |
+| `log` | `pi.log(...)` | `host_call(method=log)` | none | Structured logging only. |
+| `tool` | `pi.tool(<non-core>)` | `host_call(method=tool, name=<non-core>)` | none | Used for unknown/custom tools; forces prompt/deny in strict/prompt modes. |
+
+Notes:
+- The `fs` hostcall method is optional until the FS connector lands, but **when
+  present** it MUST map to `read`/`write` exactly as shown above.
+- The `tool` capability is a **catch-all** for non-core tools; the host should
+  prefer explicit `read`/`write`/`exec` mapping for built-ins.
+
+#### Mapping rules (required)
+
+1) **Core derives capability** from `method` + `params` (never trust extension
+   provided capability for authorization).
+2) **JS and WASM map to the same capability names**. A policy decision made for
+   JS must be identical for the equivalent WASM call.
+3) **Mismatch is an error**: if `host_call.payload.capability` disagrees with
+   the derived capability, respond with `invalid_request`.
+
+#### Policy + logging alignment
+
+- The **same policy evaluator** applies to both runtimes.
+- Audit logs **must include** `capability`, `method`, and the derived decision.
+- Recommended: include a `runtime` field in `log.data` (`js` or `wasm`) to make
+  cross-runtime comparisons trivial.
+
+---
+
 ### 3.3 Capability Manifest (`pi.ext.cap.v1`)
 
 `register.payload.capability_manifest` optionally declares the extension’s
